cards.definition: The model proposes an action, the harness executes it, and the result returns as the model's next observation.
cards.movie_element: Tony's first test flight of the Mark II, JARVIS on comms
cards.movie_element_detail: During the Mark II test flight, Tony calls the maneuver, the suit performs it, and JARVIS reports the result back — the altitude record he asks about, then the warning of a potentially fatal ice buildup — before Tony picks his next move.
cards.mapping_line: Tony calls the move, the suit flies it, JARVIS reports back — decide, act, listen, repeat.
cards.break_point: Tony also sees and feels the sky himself; the AI perceives nothing except the text results the scaffolding hands back.
cards.bridge: In real systems, software runs each requested action and pastes the outcome into the conversation as the AI's next input.

cards.definition: The only actions the model can take are the typed functions the harness exposes.
cards.movie_element: The armor's built-in arsenal in the Gulmira rescue
cards.movie_element_detail: In Gulmira, everything Tony does to the battlefield — repulsor blasts, the shoulder-mounted anti-personnel guns, flares, flight — is a function Stark built into the armor beforehand. His reach is exactly what the hardware offers.
cards.mapping_line: Suited up, Tony's entire arsenal is whatever he welded into the armor beforehand.
cards.break_point: Tony steps out of the armor and acts freely; the AI has no body outside its given functions.
cards.bridge: Tools are a fixed menu of functions the scaffolding offers; the AI can only request items from that menu.

cards.definition: A standing instruction block installed from outside that the model cannot edit.
cards.movie_element: Yinsen's dying words: 'Don't waste your life'
cards.movie_element_detail: Yinsen gives Tony the instruction in the cave as he dies. Tony did not write it and cannot unhear it, and every choice he makes afterward — shutting down the weapons division, building the suit — plays out under it.
cards.mapping_line: Tony didn't choose Yinsen's last words, but every choice he makes afterward answers to them.
cards.break_point: Tony carries the words as memory; the real instructions are re-pasted in front of the AI before every single turn.
cards.bridge: A system prompt is standing text the operator prepends to every request; the model reads it fresh each time and cannot rewrite it.

cards.definition: Separate model instances delegated tasks, each with its own context, returning only a final report.
cards.movie_element: Pepper's solo mission into the Stark mainframe
cards.movie_element_detail: Tony tasks Pepper with copying the company's shipping manifests. She goes alone and handles what she finds her own way — discovering the Sector 16 files and the ransom video, slipping past Stane, leaving under Coulson's protection.
cards.mapping_line: Tony hands Pepper one job — she does it her own way, with what she alone knows.
cards.break_point: In the film her findings flow to the agents, not to Tony; a real delegate reports back to its sender, then vanishes.
cards.bridge: Real delegates are fresh AI instances: one assigned task, a private working conversation, and a single summary sent back to the requester.

cards.definition: Before dangerous actions, the harness pauses for human approval.
cards.movie_element: JARVIS holding the Mark III render until Tony orders fabrication
cards.movie_element_detail: JARVIS presents the finished Mark III design; Tony rejects the gold as ostentatious and asks for 'a little hot rod red in there'. Nothing gets built until Tony reviews the render and gives the order to fabricate.
cards.mapping_line: The new armor stays a picture on the screen until Tony says build it.
cards.break_point: In the film the pause is JARVIS's manner; in reality it is enforced machinery the AI cannot talk its way past.
cards.bridge: Before risky actions — deleting files, sending money — the harness freezes and a human must click approve.

cards.definition: User-configured checks that fire automatically on matching actions and can block them.
cards.movie_element: Dum-E standing by with the fire extinguisher
cards.movie_element_detail: During Tony's thruster and flight-stabilizer tests, Dum-E stands by holding a fire extinguisher and sprays automatically — sometimes when Tony isn't even on fire, earning the threat: 'If you douse me again, and I'm not on fire, I'm donating you to a city college.'
cards.mapping_line: Dum-E waits with the extinguisher and sprays on his own the instant something looks like fire.
cards.break_point: Dum-E can only douse a fire after it starts; a real check can also veto the action before it ever runs.
cards.bridge: Hooks are small scripts the user registers; the harness runs them automatically around matching actions and they can cancel the action outright.

unmapped.definition: When the conversation outgrows the model's window, older turns are summarized.
unmapped.direct_explanation: When the conversation grows past what the AI can read at once, the software replaces the oldest exchanges with a short summary — detail is deliberately lost so the gist can keep fitting.
unmapped.why_no_mapping: Nothing in the film condenses accumulated information to fit a limited space; the arc reactor's miniaturization shrinks hardware, not information, which is a surface resemblance only.
